what_it_does: An acceptance test that exercises the Suite.act structured-click dispatch seam end-to-end. It builds a small graph, then sends click-shaped {verb, address, args} calls through suite.act to verify that dispatch bypasses prose parsing, that only whitelisted verbs are accepted, that propose surfaces a draft instead of self-applying, and that every outcome is re-folded into a confirmation reply.
observations: Sets COMPANY_TEST_RUN=1 at import time to tag surfaced drafts as test_origin.
Creates a temporary FsStore and a Suite, then builds a two-node constant->uppercase graph.
Defines a global PASS counter and a check(label, cond) helper that asserts and increments PASS.
Tests the show verb with address ui://chrome/inbox and checks target resolution and reply wording.
Tests the run verb and checks that the graph recomputes and routed_posture is auto.
Sends the literal string "ACTION: run" as a verb to prove it is refused as an unknown structured verb rather than prose-parsed.
Verifies that refused apply, delete, and write_file clicks leave the node store unchanged.
Tests propose surfaces a draft with routed_posture confirm, does not register a live node type, and adds an unapproved inbox item.
Tests consult returns a reply beginning with 📖 and longer than three characters.
Cleans up the temporary store directory in a try/finally block.
standouts: The explicit dispatch-bypass-prose proof using "ACTION: run" as a literal verb.
Whitelist enforcement is shown to live inside the dispatcher, not widened by the click path.
Propose is shown to surface a draft for operator approval rather than self-apply.
Consult is required to return its full answer, not a silent else-branch.
conventions: Script-style acceptance test with a global counter instead of a unittest/pytest class.
Uses tempfile.mkdtemp and shutil.rmtree for store isolation.
Mutates sys.path and os.environ at module top.
Asserts on specific human-readable reply strings and emoji prefixes.
Accesses internal Suite methods such as _load, chat_history, list_surfaced, and inbox.is_approved.
concerns: Heavy reliance on private/internal Suite methods makes the test brittle to implementation changes.
Global mutable PASS counter and assert-based check helper are not thread-safe and give limited failure context.
Hardcoded reply strings and emoji assertions will break if copy or formatting changes.
COMPANY_TEST_RUN environment mutation is not reset after the test run.
The finally block silently ignores cleanup errors.
notes: A script-style acceptance test that treats deterministic clicks as the relocated emission seam and proves the prose parser is not involved.
questions: What are the exact seven verbs in the whitelist?
How does Suite.act map an address string to the natural target slot for each verb?
What do routed_posture values auto and confirm represent in the governance model?
How does consult generate a full answer without invoking a model?
What is the precise contract between the thin /api/act route and Suite.act?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the file: it demonstrates the /api/act seam end-to-end with deterministic clicks, whitelist refusal, no-self-apply, and re-folded confirmations.
intention_signals: The file is trying to prove that the interactive act path can be driven by deterministic, human-click-shaped commands rather than unreliable model prose, while preserving the existing governance guarantees around whitelisting, confirmation, and draft approval.
novelty: It frames the click path as a relocation of the emission seam and includes an explicit negative test that a prose-looking token is refused as an unknown structured verb, demonstrating the prose parser is bypassed.
contribution: An acceptance test that records the expected Suite.act behavior for show, run, propose, and consult, documents the dispatch-bypass-prose invariant, and provides a regression check that non-whitelisted verbs are refused without side effects.
summary_for_embedding: A script-style acceptance test for the Suite.act click-shaped dispatch seam that verifies bypass of prose parsing, whitelist enforcement, no-self-apply propose behavior, and re-folded confirmations.
intention_for_embedding: To prove end-to-end that deterministic human clicks can drive the /api/act seam while preserving governance rules and avoiding prose parsing.

symbols.does: Assertion helper that increments a global PASS counter when a condition holds.
symbols.description: The check function takes a label and a boolean condition, asserts the condition with a FAIL label on failure, and otherwise increments the module-level PASS counter while printing an ok message. It is the only local test utility in this script-style acceptance test.
